notes: RPG Maker MV/MZ vertical-slice pair-policy (analogous to sweetie-hd-alpha-1). Same single (modelId, providerId) primary pair pinned for every agentic-loop stage. On the wire itotori sends provider.order=[providerId] + allow_fallbacks=true + zdr=true + data_collection=deny; OpenRouter may report a ZDR-allow-list provider different from the requested primary, which itotori records as a provider-side routing outcome without an application retry or failover. The driver bounds the live slice to ONE dialogue surface (--engine-profile rpg-maker-mv-mz translates only the targeted unit, rest no-op), so a full run bills a single ZDR translation invocation. Unlike RealLive, the translated text is NOT SJIS-bracket-wrapped: RPG Maker stores plain JSON string literals, so the real translated draft is spliced byte-surgically into one Map/CommonEvent Show-Text literal by the kaifuu-rpgmaker patchback; the utsushi-rpgmaker-mv E1 text-trace then executes the patched data and the harness asserts the engine's OBSERVED TextLine bodies reflect the real translated text (no planted sentinel substring). sceneId is carried for schema conformance but unused by the MV/MZ JSON extractor (which walks the whole www/data tree). Per-stage zdr / seed / maxPriceUsd are intentionally omitted: the parser fills canonical defaults. Each leaf explicitly declares maximumBillableCostUsd=$0.50, the operator-approved hard reservation ceiling for one paid physical invocation; maxPriceUsd is never substituted for it. Operator-level zdr downgrades require OPENROUTER_ZDR_DOWNGRADE; no file-level escape hatch.
